user_outcome: A Sworn release operator can prove the delivered direct OpenRouter structured path through a native atomic sanitized receipt, and after the historical GLM proof ends opaquely can make one explicitly governed recovery using the verifier model currently selected in Sworn config, without a model flag, payload disclosure, fallback, or unbounded retry.
rationale: S22 already delivers the direct-only OpenRouter forced-tool transport and the native fail-closed receipt facility. Historical attempts 1 and 2 are immutable: attempt 1 is receipt_failure/UNPARSEABLE with unavailable exit semantics, and attempt 2 is openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2 opaque/UNPARSEABLE with exit 2. The former contract correctly made attempt 2 terminal. Brad has now ratified a factual contract correction rather than an implicit retry: one separately governed attempt 3 may resolve verifier.model only through config.Load plus ResolveVerifierModel with an empty flag, record the resolved model ID in the same strict metadata receipt, and dispatch only after fresh Captain review and all deterministic/proof gates. Unsupported or unconfigured values fail before reservation or dispatch. This does not broaden the typed retry classifier, establish a provider/model default, reuse a CLI model override, expose config paths or provider data, or permit fallback or attempt 4. The direct OpenRouter transport, canonical schema validation, S04 requested/emitted identity authority, MCP fixed diagnostic, S21 freshness gate, and post-rename trust guard remain unchanged.
in_scope: S22 is serially gated after S21-openai-structured-envelope in T1-foundation. Before any proof-receipt dispatch, preflight must mechanically read S22's upstream_gate and the resolved S21 status, require their slice/release identity, immutable start ed0badf68673f0af84834458f07be0792555484f, authoritative-status reference, verified state, pass result, non-empty verifier verdict time, and verifier_was_fresh_context true to agree, and reject every absent, stale, or mismatched fact with zero provider dispatch.

Preserve the delivered direct-only provider split: only a direct openrouter/ model route uses StructuredToolCall and the one forced emit_structured_output function with the canonical schema as parameters. Proxy-routed OpenRouter, Ollama, generic OAI-compatible endpoints, and every unprofiled provider remain structured-output unsupported. The S21 envelope, source-schema rewrites, response_format, raw Verify, fallback, and unconstrained-text repair remain prohibited.

Add a native sworn llm-check --proof-receipt mode for a proof bound to the selected check, model, slice, release, and immutable status.start_commit. It owns the receipt lifecycle rather than accepting a shell-redirection convention. It must reject an implicit or mismatched base, an invalid receipt directory, a malformed historical receipt, a changed check/model/slice/release/start binding, a final prior verdict, an opaque prior outcome, or an exhausted budget before any provider dispatch or retry-budget consumption.

Make proof-receipt mode preflight and atomically persist a private metadata-only attempt reservation before dispatch using create, fsync, close, rename, and parent-directory sync. It must finalize the same attempt atomically to the version-appropriate strict v1 or v2 receipt. If finalization fails after a rename and restoring the reservation also fails, no final model verdict may remain trusted: the facility must retain or surface a durable receipt_failure/UNPARSEABLE record with unavailable exit semantics, fail closed, and preserve no raw payload. Receipt failure remains the only receipt-write condition eligible under the typed retry classifier.

Keep historical attempts 1 and 2 under immutable llm-check-proof-receipt-v1. Define configured attempt 3 under llm-check-proof-receipt-v2 with the same complete metadata allowlist and record_version 2 plus attempt const 3; neither schema is an LLMCheckReport. Both exclude config paths/content, endpoints, headers, request bodies, response bodies, findings, raw errors, prompts, diffs, credentials, and keys from receipts, normal output, Git, docs, and status/journal evidence.

Preserve the typed retry classifier unchanged: a locally validated PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED is final, and attempt 2 was eligible only because attempt 1 was receipt_failure. HTTP 400/401/402, unclassified, parse, schema, identity, malformed-tool, opaque, and untrusted outcomes remain non-retryable. The only post-attempt-2 dispatch authority is the separate human-ratified configured-recovery-v2 administrative gate; it does not change any classifier result and can authorize only terminal attempt 3.

Add a narrow model error classifier for the proof receipt policy that recognizes only the explicit retryable classes above. It must not reuse the broader legacy IsTransient treatment of unknown errors. Existing RunLLMCheck semantics remain unchanged outside proof-receipt mode; generic JSON must no longer serialize raw_response and a malformed generic response must not place raw content in a rendered finding or test failure output. MCP retains its in-process error/non-success semantics, but when sworn.llm_check reaches a provider/model error its public text must be the stable diagnostic llm_check: provider request failed and must contain no endpoint, header, request, response, prompt, diff, credential, key, or provider-body content.

Add deterministic local coverage with synthetic keys and local endpoints for final PASS, FAIL, and BLOCKED; 429, 5xx, normalized network/deadline, runner, and receipt failure; 400, 401, 402, parse, schema, identity, malformed-tool, opaque, and unknown failures; atomic preflight/finalization and post-rename restoration failure; mismatched receipt/S21 facts that make zero dispatches; unchanged two-attempt classifier behavior; exact configured attempt-3 historical/config binding; model-override and attempt-4 zero-dispatch rejection; MCP provider-error reachability; and request/response/config/key leak canaries across receipts and public output.

Preserve attempts 1 and 2 byte-for-byte as historical bindings. Add an explicit configured-recovery proof mode that rejects any non-empty --model override, resolves verifier.model using config.Load and ResolveVerifierModel with an empty flag, records only the resolved provider/model ID in attempt 3, and preflight-rejects an unconfigured, unsupported, incapable, stale-S21, changed-history, or already-present-attempt-3 state before provider dispatch. Config file paths, endpoints, credentials, and config contents must never enter receipts or public output.

Before the configured recovery, require this revised source implementation, deterministic tests proving config-only resolution and zero-dispatch rejection, full suite, vet, build, regenerated proof bundle, and a fresh Captain design review plus acknowledgement. Only then may the native command make exactly attempt 3 against immutable start a09b0e46df465862d00469d4aef2a997442b3d5b. Every attempt-3 outcome is terminal and no attempt 4, fallback, provider/model switch, or generic retry is permitted. A PASS still requires a fresh artefact-only S22 verifier PASS before S20 may resume; every other result leaves S22 and S20 blocked.
out_of_scope: Changing internal/baton/schemas/llm-check-report-v1.json, internal/baton/schemas/spec-ambiguity-report-v1.json, embedded schema bytes, Baton prompts, rules, or S04 semantic parser and requested/emitted-check equality is prohibited.
Changing S21's verified OpenAI envelope or applying response_format or another OpenAI transport authority to OpenRouter is prohibited.

Enabling structured generic checks through the Sworn hosted proxy for OpenRouter model IDs, Ollama, a generic OAI-compatible endpoint, or any other provider is prohibited. Each remains a separate provider-and-transport decision requiring its own plan and evidence.

Changing model defaults, configured verifier values, model catalogue or pricing, hosted-service behavior, customer key handling, or commercial policy is prohibited. GLM remains the immutable historical S22/S20 proof input; attempt 3 consumes but never edits or replaces the operator's current verifier configuration.

Adding a generic automatic retry policy, changing ordinary RunLLMCheck behavior or MCP control/error semantics beyond the specified stable public provider-error diagnostic, retaining model/provider payloads, or using error-message text as a retry classifier is prohibited.

Making a provider or model call in this Planner session, running S20 readiness/maintainability/ac-satisfaction, changing S20 source/start/semantic commits/proof, touching real Codex or Claude homes, or resuming S20 before fresh S22 verification is prohibited.

A proof-specific --model override, a fallback after config resolution, raw-text repair, a generic retry-classifier expansion, or any fourth provider call is prohibited. Attempts 1 and 2 plus the single ratified config-only attempt 3 are the complete S22 proof budget.

effort_complexity.rationale: The material re-scope crosses a public CLI proof mode, durable atomic metadata, conservative provider-error classification, output confidentiality, exact retry bounds, direct-only transport preservation, and fresh design review while retaining one immutable started slice.

risks.description: An OpenRouter-looking endpoint or proxy route accidentally receives structured capability and becomes an unreviewed transport authority.
risks.mitigation: Retain construction-time direct/proxy separation and prove proxy OpenRouter, unknown OAI, and Ollama reject before dispatch.

risks.description: A function-call adapter mutates the canonical schema or treats provider tool syntax as semantic validation.
risks.mitigation: Keep canonical parameters and S04 local validation/requested-emitted identity authority unchanged; tool transport remains below that gate.

risks.description: A malformed, arbitrary, or multiple tool call is normalized into a valid-looking generic report.
risks.mitigation: Keep exact direct tool-call cardinality/name/type/object guards and test all malformed cases as non-success with no repair or fallback.

risks.description: Raw provider data, a request body, a credential, or an endpoint reaches a receipt, normal output, Git, docs, or a test failure.
risks.mitigation: Use a strict separate receipt schema/renderer, sanitized terminal output, generic raw_response exclusion, non-raw parse diagnostics, and leak-canary assertions.

risks.description: A generic or ambiguous failure consumes an unbounded retry budget or makes a failed proof look like a model verdict.
risks.mitigation: Recognize only enumerated typed/environmental classes, reserve attempts atomically before dispatch, bind receipts to the immutable proof identity, and stop after any attempt-2 non-final outcome without a third call.

risks.description: The selected GLM proof is treated as a model default or as authority to resume S20 without independent evidence.
risks.mitigation: Keep model selection proof-only, require fresh Captain review, deterministic gates, native receipt evidence, and a fresh S22 verifier PASS before an independent later S20 smoke.

risks.description: An MCP wrapper exposes provider-response-derived text even though receipt and CLI output are sanitized.
risks.mitigation: Keep MCP failure semantics but replace its public provider-error text with one stable diagnostic and prove it with deterministic registered-tool reachability and leak-canary tests.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN NewClient constructs direct openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2 with an OpenRouter key THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve model z-ai/glm-5.2, advertise structured output only through StructuredToolCall, and keep a proxy-routed OpenRouter client, Ollama, and an unprofiled OAI-compatible client without structured-output capability (N-13).

WHEN direct OpenRouter ChatStructured receives the exact canonical llm-check-report-v1 bytes THE SYSTEM SHALL send one OpenAI-compatible nested function tool named emit_structured_output, force that exact name through tool_choice, use the unchanged canonical schema as its parameters, and omit response_format, the S21 OpenAI envelope, and any transformed schema (N-02, N-13).

WHEN a built sworn llm-check --type ac-satisfaction process runs with --model openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2, SWORN_DIRECT=1, a synthetic OpenRouter key, and SWORN_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL pointing to a deterministic fake endpoint THE SYSTEM SHALL reach the direct OpenRouter tool wire, accept a real named tool-call response containing check ac-satisfaction only through the existing local canonical gate, print a PASS report, and exit 0 (N-02, N-10, N-13).

IF direct OpenRouter returns zero, multiple, or differently named tool calls; non-object arguments other than the separately required JSON null case; a PASS with a blocking finding; a FAIL without a blocking finding; a missing check; or a check other than ac-satisfaction THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return non-success, preserve S04 local canonical validation and requested/emitted equality, make no synthetic repair or raw-text fallback, and make no second provider request (N-02, N-10, N-13).

WHEN SWORN_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL is unset THE SYSTEM SHALL retain https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 for direct OpenRouter; IF it is invalid, a Sworn proxy route is selected, or a different provider is selected THEN the override SHALL not redirect a request and the affected setup SHALL fail closed or retain its existing endpoint behavior (N-10, N-13).

WHEN a proof-receipt invocation supplies the selected check, resolved model, slice, release, and exact immutable S22 start commit THE SYSTEM SHALL preflight and atomically create a private metadata-only reservation before provider dispatch, then atomically finalize attempts 1-2 as immutable llm-check-proof-receipt-v1 or configured attempt 3 as llm-check-proof-receipt-v2 using only schema/version, release, slice ID, check type, model ID, immutable start, attempt ordinal, class, result, and exit semantics; IF reservation or finalization fails THEN the system SHALL retain or surface only receipt_failure/UNPARSEABLE, infer no model verdict, expose no payload, and make no provider request when preflight failed; IF finalization fails after rename and reservation restoration also fails THEN no final verdict SHALL remain trusted and the system SHALL retain or surface a durable receipt_failure with unavailable exit semantics; IF any persisted receipt has a mismatched version/ordinal/check/model/slice/release/start binding THEN it SHALL reject before dispatch (N-10, N-14).

IF the sole otherwise valid direct OpenRouter emit_structured_output call supplies function.arguments as JSON null THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it locally as non-success before canonical report acceptance, make no synthetic repair or fallback, and make exactly one provider request (N-02, N-10, N-13).

IF the sole otherwise valid direct OpenRouter emit_structured_output call has a type other than function THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it locally as non-success before canonical report acceptance, make no synthetic repair or fallback, and make exactly one provider request (N-02, N-10, N-13).

WHEN proof-receipt mode reads attempts 1 and 2 THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve the original typed retry rule unchanged: attempt 2 was permitted only because attempt 1 was receipt_failure, and attempt 2 remains terminal for that classifier; IF AND ONLY IF the exact immutable historical receipts are followed by the ratified configured-recovery authority, fresh Captain acknowledgement, and deterministic/proof gates THEN the system SHALL permit separately governed attempt 3 without treating attempt-2 opaque as retryable; IF any historical check, model, slice, release, start, ordinal, class, result, or exit binding differs, or attempt 3 already exists, THEN it SHALL reject before dispatch; every attempt-3 outcome SHALL be terminal with no attempt 4 (N-10, N-14).

IF a proof result is HTTP 400, 401, or 402; an unclassified error; a parse, schema, requested/emitted identity, malformed-tool, or opaque result; or a receipt whose binding is invalid THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL record a non-retryable sanitized class and never use an error message or raw content to infer retry eligibility; the sole exception SHALL be the human-ratified configured-recovery authority for historical attempt 2, which is administrative authority outside the typed classifier and may create only attempt 3 after all exact gates; no other such outcome may cause an additional provider call (N-02, N-10, N-14).

WHEN proof-receipt mode or generic llm-check JSON renders a provider outcome THE SYSTEM SHALL exclude raw_response, endpoints, headers, request/response bodies, findings, prompts, diffs, credentials, and keys from receipt files and normal output; malformed generic output SHALL render a stable non-raw diagnostic; IF sworn.llm_check reaches a provider/model error THEN MCP SHALL retain its error/non-success semantics and return exactly llm_check: provider request failed as public text, with no endpoint, header, request, response, prompt, diff, credential, key, or provider-body content (N-02, N-10, N-14).

WHEN S21-openai-structured-envelope remains freshly verified and S22 preflight mechanically confirms its declared S21 identity, immutable start, authoritative status reference, verified/pass result, non-empty verifier verdict time, and verifier_was_fresh_context true, and the configured-recovery implementation, Captain-reviewed design acknowledgement, deterministic S22 tests, full suite, vet, build, and regenerated proof bundle pass THE IMPLEMENTER MAY invoke exactly native attempt 3 with --proof-receipt --configured-recovery, no --model value, config.Load plus ResolveVerifierModel("", cfg), and immutable start a09b0e46df465862d00469d4aef2a997442b3d5b; THE SYSTEM SHALL persist the resolved configured model ID but no config path/content, endpoint, credential, or payload; IF config or S21 evidence is absent, unsupported, stale, or mismatched THEN S22 SHALL remain gated with zero proof dispatch; IF attempt 3 is PASS and a fresh artefact-only S22 verifier separately records PASS THEN and only then may S20 resume (N-10, N-13, N-14).
